mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate from extreme levels indicates the economic recovery is gaining momentum faster than anticipated, leading to upward revisions in corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by > 1.0pp in one month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE dropped from 14.7% in April 2020 to 13.3% in May 2020
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where the unemployment rate drops by more than 1.0pp month-over-month from a level above 8%.

mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate anchored near zero, low discount rates and the central bank's commitment to accommodative policy provide a continuous tailwind for equity valuations, driving positive returns as long as this policy stance is maintained.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS rate is 0.05% as of May 2020
failure_condition: The signal's rolling 3-year Sharpe ratio is negative while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rebound in real retail sales, proxied by rising nominal retail sales (RSAFS) coupled with low and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL), signals a robust recovery in consumer demand without triggering fears of monetary tightening, thus boosting corporate revenue forecasts and equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Massive fiscal stimulus (CARES Act) deployed to support consumption
failure_condition: The combined signal (high RSAFS growth, low CPIAUCSL growth) has a hit rate below 55% for positive 20-day forward returns over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production reflects the reopening of the economy and the restoration of supply chains. This improvement in the productive capacity of the economy drives expectations of higher corporate profits and positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO fell sharply in March/April 2020 due to lockdowns
failure_condition: A 3-month positive change in INDPRO is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When monetary policy is at the zero lower bound, a negative surprise in nonfarm payrolls growth reduces expectations of a premature withdrawal of stimulus, lowering long-term rates and boosting equity valuations through a lower discount rate.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has signaled commitment to supporting the labor market
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, negative monthly PAYEMS changes are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in a majority of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant contraction in housing starts indicates a severe loss of confidence among builders and consumers, signaling a sharp downturn in a key cyclical sector that has broad spillover effects on the economy, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST fell from 1567k in Jan 2020 to 891k in April 2020
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances following a 3-month drop in HOUST of over 20%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a decline in industrial production (INDPRO) creates a stagflationary environment. This condition erodes corporate profit margins and limits the central bank's ability to provide stimulus, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of this combined signal for 60-day forward returns is not statistically significantly negative (p > 0.10) over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: The rate of change of real GDP is a direct measure of aggregate economic growth. A higher growth rate translates directly into higher aggregate corporate earnings growth, which in turn drives positive long-term equity market returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2020 GDPC1 showed a sharp contraction of -5.1% annualized
failure_condition: The correlation between quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is not positive and significant over a full business cycle.
